intro: The clearest split is that Ably uses a subscription model and Dyte uses a freemium model. Ably is Real-time messaging infrastructure with pub/sub, presence, and 99.999% uptime SLA across global PoPs; Dyte is Video call and audio room SDK with custom UI components, recording, and live streaming. The data points Ably at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Dyte at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Ably is subscription from $29 / month; Dyte is freemium from $0.00. Lock-in is low for Ably and medium for Dyte, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Ably emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Dyte emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Only Ably lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Ably is for chat applications; Dyte is for chat applications; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Ably if a subscription pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Dyte if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $29 / month for Ably and $0.00 for Dyte. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Ably to Dyte?
faq.a: Ably is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary pub/sub protocol and presence model). Dyte is tagged with medium lock-in (sdk tied but data portable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Ably or Dyte?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Dyte a good alternative to Ably?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Dyte is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
